popcycles 0.0.0.9000

    • The Hippos of Pablo Escobar
    • Rabbit population growth
  • Changelog

    Rabbit population growth

    Nathan Brouwer

    2018-07-29

    rabbit_examples.Rmd

    Preliminaries

    library(ggplot2)
#> Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 3.5.1
library(cowplot)
#> 
#> Attaching package: 'cowplot'
#> The following object is masked from 'package:ggplot2':
#> 
#>     ggsave
library(popcycles)

    Exponential population growth

    No limits on population growth

    Project population 10 years into future

    N.rabbit.exp <- logit_discrete_multistep(alpha = 0, 
                                K = NULL,
                                r.d = 1,
                                time.steps = 10,
                                Nt = 10)

    Plot exponential populatin growth

    gg_plot_logit(N.rabbit.exp,
              title = "Exponential population growth in rabbits") +
xlim(0,20) +
ylim(0,3000) 
#> Warning: Removed 2 rows containing missing values (geom_path).

    Logitic population growth

    Project population 10 years into future

    N.rabbit.logit <- logit_discrete_multistep(alpha = NULL, 
                                K = 3000,
                                r.d = 1,
                                time.steps = 20,
                                Nt = 10)

    Plot exponential populatin growth

    gg_plot_logit(N.rabbit.logit,
              title = "Logistic population growth in rabbits") +
xlim(0,20) +
ylim(0,3000)

    Overlay the two growth curves

    Project population 10 years into future

    N.rabbit.exp.20yr <- logit_discrete_multistep(alpha = 0, 
                                K = NULL,
                                r.d = 1,
                                time.steps = 20,
                                Nt = 10)
    N.rabbit.exp.20yr$growth.type <- "exponential"
N.rabbit.logit$growth.type <- "logistic"

N.rabbit.compare <- rbind(N.rabbit.exp.20yr,
                          N.rabbit.logit)

axis.txt.sz<-25
axis.ti.sz<-25
title.sz <- 30
title <- "Exponential vs. logistic"
ggplot(aes(y = N.t,
             x = time),
         data = N.rabbit.compare) +
    #geom_point(size = 4) +
    geom_line(size = 3,
              aes(color =growth.type ,
                  linetype = growth.type)) +
    xlab("Time (t)") +
    ylab("Population size (N)") + 
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=axis.txt.sz),
        axis.title=element_text(size=axis.ti.sz,face="bold")) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(size=title.sz)) +
    ggtitle(title) +
ylim(0,5000)
#> Warning: Removed 12 rows containing missing values (geom_path).

    Focus on part where logit begins to curve

    ggplot(aes(y = N.t,
             x = time),
         data = N.rabbit.compare) +
    #geom_point(size = 4) +
    geom_line(size = 3,
              aes(color =growth.type ,
                  linetype = growth.type)) +
    xlab("Time (t)") +
    ylab("Population size (N)") + 
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=axis.txt.sz),
        axis.title=element_text(size=axis.ti.sz,face="bold")) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(size=title.sz)) +
   # ggtitle(title) +
ylim(0,3000) +
xlim(7,15)
#> Warning: Removed 31 rows containing missing values (geom_path).

    Logitic population growth with various parameters

    Project population 10 years into future

    N.rabbit.logit.K3000 <- logit_discrete_multistep(alpha = NULL, 
                                K = 3000,
                                r.d = 1,
                                time.steps = 20,
                                Nt = 10)

    Plot exponential populatin growth

    gg_plot_logit(N.rabbit.logit.K3000,
              title = "") +
xlim(0,20) +
ylim(0,6000)

    Vary K 2x

    N.rabbit.logit.K6000 <- logit_discrete_multistep(alpha = NULL, 
                                K = 6000,
                                r.d = 1,
                                time.steps = 20,
                                Nt = 10)

    Plot K3000 vs K600

    K3kvsK6K <- rbind(N.rabbit.logit.K3000,
                  N.rabbit.logit.K6000)
ggplot(aes(y = N.t,
             x = time),
         data = K3kvsK6K) +
    #geom_point(size = 4) +
    geom_line(size = 3,
              aes(color = factor(K) ,
                  linetype = factor(K))) +
    xlab("Time (t)") +
    ylab("Population size (N)") + 
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=axis.txt.sz),
        axis.title=element_text(size=axis.ti.sz,face="bold")) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(size=title.sz)) +
    #ggtitle(title) +
ylim(0,6000)

    Vary r 1.5

    N.rabbit.logit.r1.5 <- logit_discrete_multistep(alpha = NULL, 
                                K = 3000,
                                r.d = 1*1.5,
                                time.steps = 20,
                                Nt = 10)

    Plot K3000 vs K600

    N.rabbit.logit.K3000$r <- 1
N.rabbit.logit.r1.5$r <- 1.5
K3kvsK6K <- rbind(N.rabbit.logit.K3000,
                  N.rabbit.logit.r1.5)
ggplot(aes(y = N.t,
             x = time),
         data = K3kvsK6K) +
    #geom_point(size = 4) +
    geom_line(size = 3,
              aes(color = factor(r) ,
                  linetype = factor(r))) +
    xlab("Time (t)") +
    ylab("Population size (N)") + 
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=axis.txt.sz),
        axis.title=element_text(size=axis.ti.sz,face="bold")) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(size=title.sz)) +
    #ggtitle(title) +
ylim(0,6000)

    Vary r 2x

    N.rabbit.logit.r2 <- logit_discrete_multistep(alpha = NULL, 
                                K = 3000,
                                r.d = 1*2,
                                time.steps = 20,
                                Nt = 10)

    Plot K3000 vs K600

    N.rabbit.logit.K3000$r <- 1
N.rabbit.logit.r2$r <- 2
K3kvsK6K <- rbind(N.rabbit.logit.K3000,
                  N.rabbit.logit.r2)
ggplot(aes(y = N.t,
             x = time),
         data = K3kvsK6K) +
    #geom_point(size = 4) +
    geom_line(size = 3,
              aes(color = factor(r) ,
                  linetype = factor(r))) +
    xlab("Time (t)") +
    ylab("Population size (N)") + 
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=axis.txt.sz),
        axis.title=element_text(size=axis.ti.sz,face="bold")) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(size=title.sz)) +
    #ggtitle(title) +
ylim(0,6000)
